channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: U.S. Ambassador Denies Link Between Moroun Family Donation and Gordie Howe Bridge Delay
item.description: The article details U.S. Ambassador Ron Klain's denial of claims that a $1 million donation from the Moroun family is connected to delays in opening the Gordie Howe International Bridge. Klain asserts...

item.title: Kenya's LSK Calls for Urgent, Impartial Probe into Advocate's Murder in Athi River
item.description: The Law Society of Kenya (LSK) has called for a swift, thorough, and impartial investigation into the murder of advocate Edward Muthee Kariuki, who was found dead outside his residence in Athi River o...

item.title: Officer Fatally Shot During Protests at World Cup Event
item.description: A Dallas police officer was fatally shot during protests related to the 2026 FIFA World Cup, sparking widespread concern about public safety and law enforcement protocols. The incident occurred in a d...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Continues Inquiry into Justice System Allegations
item.description: The Madlanga commission of inquiry, which is investigating allegations of criminality, political interference, and corruption within the criminal justice system, continues its hearings on Wednesday. O...

item.title: Kesi Constituency By-Election Proceeds as Voters Cast Their Votes
item.description: The parliamentary by-election for Kesi constituency, previously represented by the late Baba Levo, is ongoing as voters elect a new representative. The vacancy arose after Baba Levo's death, necessita...

item.title: Iyabo Ojo's decision to include ex-husband at daughter's wedding highlights focus on children's well-being
item.description: Nollywood actress Iyabo Ojo shared her reasons for allowing her ex-husband, Ademidun Ojo, to be a prominent figure at their daughter Priscilla Ojo's wedding. The couple divorced in 2001 after marrying...

item.title: Mumbai must recognise extreme rainfall as the new normal and strengthen climate resilience
item.description: The article discusses how Mumbai’s recent intense rainfall events highlight a growing climate crisis that the city’s administration is failing to adequately address. In a week of continuous heavy rain...

item.title: House prosecutors request expanded BIR records for VP Sara Duterte's impeachment case
item.description: The House prosecution panel has formally requested the Senate impeachment court to subpoena an expanded list of Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) records related to Vice President Sara Duterte, her hus...

item.title: UK Fighter Jets Intercept Russian Maritime Patrol Aircraft in Norwegian Sea
item.description: UK fighter jets intercepted a Russian maritime patrol aircraft after it 'repeatedly approached' a carrier strike group in the Norwegian Sea, according to the Ministry of Defence (MoD). The Russian Bea...

item.title: Senator Cruz and Trump's Involvement in FIFA Corruption Case
item.description: U.S. Senator Ted Cruz (R-TX) and President Donald Trump faced significant backlash after Cruz openly acknowledged Trump's role in overturning a red card penalty for U.S. national team soccer player Fo...

item.title: Pakistan Army Commits to Ensuring Water Share Availability Amid Treaty Concerns
item.description: The article discusses the commitment of Pakistan's army leadership to ensure the availability of Pakistan's rightful share of water, amid concerns over the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) and India's unilat...

item.title: India's R&D Fund Needs Legal Framework for Transparency and IP Rights
item.description: The Indian government's Rs 1 lakh crore R&D fund aims to boost innovation but faces challenges in transparency and intellectual property management. The article highlights how public funding for resea...

item.title: Subaru Driver Fled After Alleged Hit-and-Run in Nairobi
item.description: A Subaru driver allegedly fled the scene of an accident involving a motorcyclist in Ngumba Estate, Nairobi, prompting a chaotic high-speed chase. The incident occurred when the driver, whose vehicle w...

item.title: Gauteng Premier Addresses Families of Ratanda Protest Victims Amid Water Crisis
item.description: Gauteng Premier Panyaza Lesufi met with families of two men killed during violent service delivery protests at Ratanda in the Lesedi Local Municipality. The protests, which erupted due to water outage...

item.title: Toronto street vendors face FIFA branding enforcement challenges
item.description: Street vendors in Toronto are navigating strict FIFA branding rules as authorities crack down on counterfeit merchandise. Jose Gallardo, a vendor in Etobicoke, faced police action after selling jersey...

item.title: Burundi hosts regional celebrations marking World Kiswahili Day and language development forum
item.description: Burundi is currently hosting the 5th World Kiswahili Day celebrations in its capital Bujumbura, bringing together language experts, government officials, researchers, and regional stakeholders from ac...

item.title: Decline in Daily Social Interaction Among Americans
item.description: A recent study reveals that Americans of all ages are spending significantly less time socializing compared to two decades ago. According to data from the American Time Use Survey, the average daily s...

item.title: Trump defends FIFA phone call that led to US soccer player's suspension being lifted
item.description: President Donald Trump has defended his decision to make a direct phone call to FIFA President Gianni Infantino, which resulted in the reversal of a one-game World Cup suspension for US Men’s National...

item.title: Nigeria Customs Surpasses 2025 Revenue Target Amid Fiscal Challenges
item.description: The Nigeria Customs Service (NCS) has surpassed its 2025 revenue target of N1.8 trillion, collecting over N2.3 trillion despite ongoing fiscal policies that include tax waivers and duty reductions. Th...

item.title: Albanese's Pacific Strategy and Regional Diplomacy
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese is intensifying Australia's engagement with Pacific nations through a series of high-profile meetings and strategic alliances. During a busy week, he will meet leaders ...
